text.title: Abuse Case Test Cookbook Procedure
text.summary: 4-step procedure to apply Abuse Case Test Cookbook end-to-end. Each step has named input, action, and output. Decision gates reference rules from `01-core-rules.xml`.

step.name: name-targets-and-owners
step.input: OWASP category list (IDOR, SSRF, CSRF, JWT misuse) scoped to the app
step.action: List the concrete endpoints/flows each abuse case targets; assign one named owner per abuse case; record the deadline for the recipe to land in CI.

step.name: draft-abuse-case
step.action: For each target, write the abuse-case test recipe: precondition, malicious input, expected defence, assertion. Include a failing-first version (defence disabled) to prove the test fires.

step.name: review-and-wire-into-ci
step.action: Owner reviewer signs off (r4). Wire each abuse-case test into CI with a named exit_criterion (r1) and runbook link.

step.name: log-deviation-and-route-to-retro
step.action: Any deviation from the playbook (skipped abuse-case, downgraded severity) is logged with a one-line rationale (r3) and surfaced in the next retro.

section.title: Decision gates
section.p: Between steps, consult `content/06-decision-tree.xml` if the observed signal does not match expected output. The decision tree always terminates at a rule id from `01-core-rules.xml` or at `skip-this-methodology`.
